page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 2560 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory performance and moderate multi-core compute capabilities. The server achieves a top-tier rating in large-block memory write bandwidth at 170.21 GB/sec, alongside a strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 339.94 GB/sec. The Passmark memory score of 2944.76 also ranks in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 46301.39 and a Passmark CPU score of 37081.08, both falling into the average tier. This performance profile reflects the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention compared to dedicated CPU instances.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 combined with a spacious 2560 GB NVMe SSD provides a dense resource profile for memory-intensive and storage-heavy applications without the premium of dedicated-core pricing. This makes the server well-suited for workloads such as database operations, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local storage rather than sustained, peak CPU performance.
description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on the AMD EPYC-Rome x86_64 architecture. It features a shared CPU allocation model running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 2560 GB local NVMe SSD. While its multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as a Passmark CPU score of 37081.08, place it in the average tier, its memory subsystem delivers strong performance, highlighted by a top-tier large-block write bandwidth of 170.21 GB/sec. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage make it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, static web serving, and memory-bound data processing workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is a memory-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 2560 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory write bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2560 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity NVMe storage and exceptional memory write bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
128.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
2560 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier large-block memory write bandwidth of 170.21 GB/sec
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and static web serving
